summary.what: Wire provenance into gate path + status counts
summary.why: Phase 5 is the change with user-visible behavior: the gate now silently skips foreign-author and stale commits. Hit the predictable test-fixture bomb when I first ran 'just check' — ledger.NewStorage was loading provenance from the host's user.email, causing every test that constructed mock commits without matching AuthorEmail to fail. The fix went through two iterations: first attempt (reset s.provenance in newTestStorage) only covered the ledger package's tests; second attempt (move LoadProvenanceConfig out of NewStorage into NewDefaultStorage) cleanly separates the test and production paths.
summary.how: Added classifyByProvenance calls in two places: filterByRules (the gate-path filter) and countAutoSkipped (the status counter). Both placed AFTER infra and identity checks to preserve precedence — a documented or acked commit still wins. NewStorage no longer auto-loads provenance (returns zero-config Storage); NewDefaultStorage is the production entry point that calls LoadProvenanceConfig. Exposed Storage.SetProvenance as the public way for tests and external callers to pin the config. Replaced the existing 'store.provenance = ...' direct-field-poke in provenance_test.go with SetProvenance for the precedence test. Two new tests in provenance_gate_test.go cover the gate-decision matrix (4 buckets → 1 blocking) and the status count (2 skipped of 3).

notes: Considered keeping NewStorage as the auto-loading constructor and adding a NewStorageForTest variant — rejected because Storage already has multiple test entry points across packages (mcp, cmd/timbers tests, ledger tests) and adding yet another variant would force changes in 10+ places. The NewStorage/NewDefaultStorage split mirrors the existing pattern (raw vs production-wired) and minimizes test churn. Discovered the host-config-inheritance failure mode at the right time: phase 5 is exactly where it would surface, and it's better to find it now than during the prime semantics work in phase 7.
